cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the question and the passage carefully to figure out the correct answer. The question asks which description about Yves Gomes is true. We need to choose between options A, B, C, and D. First, let's go through each option and cross-check with the passage. Option A: He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. In the passage, it says, "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old." So he left India when he was 14 months old, but the question is about where he was born. The passage doesn't explicitly say he was born in India, but the implication is that he was born in the US since he grew up there and is now an illegal immigrant. Let me check again. The passage says he is one of the "young illegal immigrants who grew up in the United States." So he was not born in India, but likely in the US. His family left India when he was 14 months old, but that would mean they were returning to India, not leaving from there. So option A is incorrect because he was not born in India. Option B: His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago. The passage says, "They were deported more than a year ago." The "they" refers to Yves Gomes' parents. But the country of deportation is the U.S., not India. They were deported from the U.S. to India, not leaving India. So his parents were in the U.S. illegally and were deported back to India more than a year ago. Option B is incorrect because it's not about them leaving India, but being deported from the U.S. to India. Option C: He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation. The passage clearly states, "He wants to finish school and become a doctor. He says without the DREAMAct, many students' dreams will be lost... He wants to finish school and eventually become a US citizen." There's no mention of wanting to become a nurse in India. He wants to become a doctor in the US. So option C is incorrect. Option D: He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct. The passage says, "Yves Gomes hopes that won't happen [referring to the DREAMAct not passing]. He wants to finish school and eventually become a US citizen." The DREAMAct is the legislation that would give him a path to legal residency and citizenship if he completes college or joins the military.
